refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation and task execution efficiency.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and outcome-focused; refining it into sub-FRs clarifies specific functional aspects that contribute to productivity, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and compare against the customer-defined target; verify sub-FRs by assessing equipment operation efficiency and task execution metrics.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time productivity feedback to operators and tele-operators.

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to maximize operational throughput.
DP2: Task scheduling and dispatch system integrated with worksite management.
DP3: Real-time telemetry and feedback interface for productivity monitoring.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to maximize operational throughput.

potential_sources: Interaction between control algorithms and task scheduling
Dependency on telemetry data for feedback
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled from task scheduling and telemetry feedback but requires well-defined interfaces to avoid tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensor data for control algorithms.
Risk of suboptimal performance if task scheduling is not aligned.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to manage coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for equipment operation and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational data and performance metrics to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives scheduled tasks and commands from higher-level modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as control algorithms are embedded in vehicle systems; low diffusion risk.
Broader scope; less focused containment for control algorithms.
Primarily for remote control, not direct control algorithm implementation.
Safety focus; minimal relevance to productivity control algorithms.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Embedding adaptive control algorithms within the Vehicle module ensures tight control over operational parameters and minimizes interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential integration complexity with task scheduling systems outside Vehicle.
Risk of insufficient interface definition leading to coupling with Teleoperation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts for Tasking and Telemetry to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by providing adaptive control to maximize throughput.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if control logic is duplicated outside Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment scores, indicating good packaging of DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring productivity metrics and validating control algorithm performance within Vehicle.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of control functionality to Vehicle module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface specifications for Tasking and Telemetry to ensure robust integration.
Plan for subsequent decomposition of task scheduling and feedback functions.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and inclusion of additional DPs for task scheduling and feedback are needed to fully address productivity requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time productivity feedback to operators and tele-operators.

revise.why: To explicitly include all functional aspects contributing to productivity and enable comprehensive DP and IM mapping.

assumptions: Control algorithms can be embedded within Vehicle module.
Interfaces Telemetry and Tasking are sufficient for module communication.
open_risks: Integration complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
Potential coupling between control and scheduling functions.
evidence_needed: Performance data from prototype control algorithms.
Interface definition documents for Tasking and Telemetry.
